headline: GE Aerospace Faces Rare Earth Reset As U.S. Rules Tighten
summary: U.S. procurement rules will prohibit Chinese origin rare earth materials in defense applications from January 2027. China currently supplies a large share of global rare earth alloys used in aerospace engines and components. Domestic U.S. rare earth projects are scaling up to supply companies such as GE Aerospace, part of General Electric (NYSE:GE). General Electric (NYSE:GE), trading at $283.57, sits at the center of this supply chain reset through its GE Aerospace business, which depends...

headline: GE HealthCare Just Crashed 13% on a Guidance Cut. Here’s the Case for Buying the Dip
summary: GE HealthCare (NASDAQ: GEHC) currently trades at $59.49, well below the Wall Street consensus price target of $89.74. That implies upside of roughly 50.8% if the analysts are right. GE HealthCare is the medical imaging and diagnostics business spun from General Electric, with about $20.6 billion in annual revenue across Advanced Imaging Solutions, Patient Care ...
